messages.content: Implement signup-form validation in fp-ts with strict types — collect ALL field errors across email, password, and username validators (not just the first). Errors should be a ReadonlyNonEmptyArray.
What's the fp-ts way to validate a checkout payload so that every bad field is reported at once? Show a minimal example.
Show me the fp-ts equivalent of piling up `E.chain` calls for profile-update validation when you actually want every error, not just the first. Brief example.
Explain briefly how validation accumulation works in fp-ts, then show a working typed implementation for a config file with port/host/timeoutMs fields.
Build a POST /orders request-body validator in fp-ts that collects every field error (customerId, items, shippingAddress) into a non-empty array.
Write a fp-ts validator for a submitted Order that accumulates all broken business rules. Constraints: use ApplicativeValidation; at least three rules; typed error channel visible in the return.

Refactor this fp-ts validator to accumulate errors instead of short-circuiting.

```ts
const validate = (ev: Event) => pipe(checkId(ev.id), E.chain(() => checkTs(ev.ts)), E.chain(() => checkType(ev.type)))
```
Explain briefly how `E.getApplicativeValidation` differs from the default Either applicative, then implement an address-form validator (street, city, postalCode).
Write a fp-ts validator for a PATCH /profile body. Constraints: three field checks (name, email, phone); use `sequenceS`; error type `ReadonlyNonEmptyArray<FieldError>` visible in the signature.

Refactor this payroll validator to accumulate errors.

```ts
const validate = (p: Payroll) => pipe(salary(p.salary), E.chain(() => hours(p.hours)), E.chain(() => dept(p.department)))
```
How do I validate a newsletter-signup form (email, firstName, zip) in fp-ts so that I get all three possible errors back at once?
Briefly explain why `E.chain` is the wrong tool for config validation, then show an accumulating validator for a job-queue config (redisUrl, concurrency, retryLimit).

Implement a loan-application validator in fp-ts with strict types — no `any`, typed `ReadonlyNonEmptyArray<FieldError>` result, accumulate every broken rule across income, debtRatio, and employmentYears.
Show me the fp-ts equivalent of writing a short-circuiting Either tower for webhook payload validation when you want all three field errors at once (eventType, occurredAt, signature). Brief example.

Add an accumulating validator to this fp-ts subscription creator so the four field errors (planId, startDate, couponCode, seats) all come back together.

```ts
type CreateSubscription = { planId: string; startDate: string; couponCode: string; seats: number }
```
Implement a questionnaire-submission validator in fp-ts with strict types and explicit accumulation across answer, consent, and submissionToken.

Write a fp-ts rebate-eligibility validator. Constraints: at least three business rules; each rule returns `Either<RNEA<FieldError>, Input>`; final result must still expose `ReadonlyNonEmptyArray<FieldError>` on the left.
What's the fp-ts way to validate a coupon-creation admin form (code, discountPct, expiresAt) and surface every field error at once?
Build a device-registration payload validator in fp-ts that accumulates all field errors (deviceId, firmwareVersion, region).
Build a signup-form validator in Effect-TS v3 that accumulates ALL per-field errors (email, password, displayName) using `Effect.either` + `Effect.all` so no field is short-circuited.
What's the Effect v3 way to validate a checkout payload with `Effect.partition` so I get both the list of field errors AND the list of successes in one shot? Minimal example.

Implement a profile-update validator in Effect-TS v3 with strict types — accumulate per-field errors (displayName, bio, avatarUrl) into `ReadonlyArray<FieldError>` using `Effect.either` + `Effect.all`.
Explain briefly why `Effect.all` is wrong for config validation, then show an accumulating Effect v3 implementation for a logging config (level, destination, format).
Write an Effect v3 validator for POST /posts. Constraints: title, body, tags checks; accumulate every failure into `ReadonlyArray<FieldError>`; success type is the same input shape.

Refactor this Effect v3 order-rule checker to accumulate every broken rule instead of stopping on the first.

```ts
const check = (o: Order) => Effect.all([minTotal(o), allowedCountry(o), itemCap(o)])
```
What's the Effect v3 idiom for validating a batch of incoming events in parallel so every bad event ends up in the error list instead of aborting on the first? Minimal example.

Add a fourth check (`termsAccepted`) to this Effect v3 account validator while keeping full error accumulation across all four fields.

```ts
const validateAccount = (a: Account) => Effect.all([Effect.either(age(a.age)), Effect.either(country(a.country)), Effect.either(kyc(a.kycStatus))])
```
Implement a strict-typed Effect v3 validator for a PATCH /users body (email, displayName, locale). Final signature must read `Effect<Body, ReadonlyArray<FieldError>>`.

Explain briefly what `Effect.forEach(items, fn, { discard: false })` does differently from a plain for-loop, then show an Effect v3 validator for a three-field tls config (certPath, keyPath, minVersion).
Build an Effect v3 business-rule validator for a subscription change (newPlan, requestedAt, paymentMethodId) that accumulates every broken rule.
Show me the Effect v3 equivalent of chaining `Effect.flatMap` for event-payload validation when you really want all failures. Brief example.
Implement an Effect v3 signup validator with strict types using `Effect.forEach` + `Effect.either` to accumulate errors from username, email, and password validators.
Write an Effect v3 checkout validator. Constraints: at least three field checks; use `Effect.partition` over a rule list; the result exposes `Effect<Checkout, ReadonlyArray<FieldError>>`.

Refactor this Effect v3 KYC validator to accumulate errors instead of failing fast.

```ts
const validateKyc = (k: Kyc) => Effect.all([ssn(k.ssn), dob(k.dob), idImage(k.idImage)])
```
How do I validate a three-field feature-flag config in Effect v3 so I get all the field errors at once without reaching for a schema library?
What's the Effect v3 way to validate three HTTP request headers (x-request-id, content-type, authorization) and return every missing/malformed one at once?
Write an Effect v3 event-ingestion validator. Constraints: accept a list of events; per-event check covers id, ts, kind; use `Effect.partition` so every bad event is surfaced alongside the good ones.
Explain briefly why `Effect.all` is wrong for validating a form, then show a working Effect v3 implementation for (firstName, lastName, email).

Add an accumulating `expectedRevision` business rule to this Effect v3 order validator while keeping the other three rules' errors visible in one shot.

```ts
const rules = [minRule, countryRule, itemsRule]
const validate = (o: Order) => Effect.validateAll(rules, (r) => r(o))
```
